response.justification: The role's hard minimum is 8 years of C++ programming, but the candidate has only roughly six months of professional C++ (National Instruments, 2014–2015) plus a university thesis. The bulk of their career is web/full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. They do bring genuinely relevant large-scale distributed-systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and strong testing/craftsmanship habits, but the central C++ and cloud-compute-platform requirements are not met. The gap on the primary qualification is too large for this mid-senior C++ leadership position.
strengths: Distributed-systems work at scale: low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests/second
Strong testing and software-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, workshops)
Broad full-stack/infra breadth including Docker, Kubernetes, and AWS CDK
concerns: Only ~6 months of C++ experience versus the 8-year hard minimum
No production cloud-compute-platform architecture or formal cloud/DevOps leadership role
Core stack is web (TS/Node/DLang), misaligned with a C++ systems platform role
